cognitive deficits resulting from the CVA. The patient's condition has resulted in 
decreased function in activities of daily living (specifically bathing, dressing, grooming, 
hygiene, and toileting). The loss of function requires assistive devices to enable the 
patient to compensate for the loss of function and maximize safety and independence. 
The patient also needs equipment such as himi-slings to prevent shoulder subluxation and 
a hand splint to prevent joint contracture and deformity
